title: Is BrainThrive right for your child?
intro: Seven quick questions about what's really going on at home. There are no wrong answers — this just helps us understand whether our drug-free, brain-based approach might be a good fit for your family.

result.aboveHeading: Your answers line up with the families we help most.
result.aboveBody: There's a real pattern here — the kind of persistent, day-to-day struggle we see all the time. The honest next step is a no-obligation consultation. We'll talk through what's going on and whether a qEEG brain map makes sense, so we're working from what's actually happening in the brain instead of guessing.
result.belowHeading: You're paying attention early — and that's a good thing.
result.belowBody: Your answers don't show the high-distress patterns we usually see when families reach out. Things sound relatively manageable right now. If that changes, or if you'd just like a clearer picture of what's going on, we're always happy to talk it through.

questions.text: What's the most disruptive thing going on right now?

answers.text: Sleep — falling asleep, staying asleep, both

questions.text: How long has this been going on?
Has anyone formally diagnosed your child?

answers.text: Yes — something else (anxiety, autism, learning, etc.)
No, and I'm not sure what's going on

questions.text: What do mornings look like at your house?
What have you already tried? Pick anything that applies.

answers.text: Nothing yet — we're still figuring it out
